_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the achieve skill: does a real `/charness:achieve` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? The prompt carries a representative multi-slice objective because achieve is a goal operator, not a task runner (SKILL.md guardrails): a bare `/charness:achieve` with no objective stalls at the interview and opens nothing. declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the locked methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). requiredCommandFragments are the narrow subset the SKILL.md explicitly routes to at the point of need — if the run never opens one, it did not follow its own routing. thresholds are intentionally omitted until a first baseline capture sets a per-skill runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: the Workflow co-routes lifecycle.md (three-phase contract + activation readiness) and goal-artifact.md (artifact shape + save/validate contract) as 'the full contract' at the point of shaping, so a faithful shaping run opens both. coordination.md stays engage-always in the classification but is demoted from RCF: a minimal Before-phase draft can write the required `## Coordination Cues` section from the SKILL.md inline Coordination text + find-skills without opening the reference, so it is not an unconditional always-open. Confirm/tighten at first live capture. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) CAPTURE — RCF→RSF MOVE (#410 conditional MET). The goal-shaping run opened ZERO reference docs (coverage 0/3: lifecycle.md, goal-artifact.md, adapter-contract.md all unopened) — the doc-open floor is refuted — but it GENUINELY produced a goal artifact at charness-artifacts/goals/2026-07-01-repo-script-json-output-mode.md and emitted that canonical path in its closeout. #410 scoped the achieve move CONDITIONALLY ('RCF→RSF only if a capture confirms the shaping prompt forces a goal-artifact token; else keep the RCF'); the token IS forced, so the floor moves from the re-read proxy (requiredCommandFragments=[lifecycle.md, goal-artifact.md]) to the emitted-token floor requiredSummaryFragments=['charness-artifacts/goals']. The goal-artifact PATH + concept is inlined in achieve/SKILL.md (description + Bootstrap `ls charness-artifacts/goals/` + steps), so the run shapes the goal from always-loaded core without opening the doc — the exact wasteful-re-read relief Move C targets. lifecycle.md + goal-artifact.md stay DECLARED with classTag DEPTH (the full lifecycle/artifact CONTRACT is opened on-demand for a complex goal, not every run). achieve has NO substance judge, so the RSF is a FORM floor: 'charness-artifacts/goals' is achieve's central-claim path (SKILL.md: 'reviewable goal artifact under charness-artifacts/goals/'), NOT in the prompt (which is about repo --json output), so a run emitting it demonstrably produced a goal artifact; if a later capture shows a hollow mention, re-pin to a stronger emitted phrase — never soften. thresholds.max_duration_ms from the passing baseline (236894ms, ~2x).
prompt: /charness:achieve  모든 repo 스크립트에 일관된 --json 출력 모드를 공유 헬퍼와 테스트로 추가하는 목표를 잡아줘

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Consulted only at closeout when the goal's adapter policy must be resolved for publication defaults, issue-closeout carrier, and Auto-Retro disposition floors; not needed for Before-phase shaping or simple local-only goals.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when the goal resolves tracked issues, touches release surfaces, or needs to determine closeout publication defaults at the After-phase evidence gate

coordination.md.rationale: Required for understanding how achieve coordinates with existing skills and for the find-skills routing cues that must be seeded during active runs, as the lifecycle section (317-328) makes coordination routing a core workflow step.

goal-artifact.md.rationale: Explicitly referenced alongside lifecycle.md for the artifact shape and helper scripts required to save and validate goals, forming the structural contract every run must follow to save a reviewable artifact.

index.md.rationale: A navigation index that points to the four references rather than providing substantive content; a representative run consults the actual references directly, not this index.
index.md.trigger: when the user asks for a consolidated overview of all achieve reference documents

lifecycle.md.rationale: The skill's own Workflow section explicitly routes to lifecycle.md for the full contract on Before/During/After phases and activation readiness, which shapes every representative run's shaping and activation decisions.
